provisions.description: Establishes congressional findings that the U.S. Forest Service caused the Hermit's Peak/Calf Canyon Fire through prescribed burns on federal land, that the fire destroyed federal, state, local, tribal, and private property, and that the United States should compensate victims. Declares the purposes of the Act are to compensate victims and provide for expeditious consideration and settlement of claims.
provisions.raw_text: Congress finds that— (1) on April 6, 2022, the Forest Service initiated the Las Dispensas-Gallinas prescribed burn on Federal land in the Santa Fe National Fo
notes: Establishes factual and legal basis for federal compensation program
Forest Service accepted responsibility for the fire
Fire resulted from prescribed burn on April 6, 2022 and pile burn holdover reemerging April 19, 2022, merging April 27, 2022
Major disaster declaration issued May 4, 2022 for Colfax, Mora, and San Miguel counties, NM

provisions.description: Establishes that each injured person shall be eligible to receive compensation from the United States for injury resulting from the Hermit's Peak/Calf Canyon Fire, subject to availability of appropriations. Creates the Office of Hermit's Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Claims within FEMA to receive, process, and pay claims. Authorizes the Office to appoint temporary personnel without competitive service requirements and reimburse other agencies. Grants the Administrator the option to appoint an Independent Claims Manager to head the Office.

provisions.description: Directs the Administrator to investigate, consider, adjust, determine, grant, deny, or settle claims on behalf of the United States. Applies New Mexico state law to the calculation of damages. Limits payments to actual compensatory damages and excludes pre-settlement interest and punitive damages.

provisions.description: Establishes claims payment procedures: Administrator must determine and fix claim amounts within 180 days of submission. Requires priority for individual claimants over insurance subrogation claims. Mandates reduction of payments by insurance benefits or other payments received. Authorizes advance or partial payments before final settlement. Enumerates allowable damages for loss of property (including acequia systems, reforestation, subsistence losses), business losses (interruption, overhead, wages, net income), and financial losses (mortgage costs, insurance deductibles, temporary living expenses, lost wages, debris removal, wildfire risk reduction, flood insurance premiums required due to the fire, SBA disaster loans).
provisions.policy_area: Disaster relief / claims payment and allowable damages

Flood insurance premiums covered only through May 31, 2024
Administrator has discretion to add 'other loss' categories

provisions.description: Provides injured persons with three options for seeking compensation: (A) submitting a claim under this Act, (B) filing under the Federal Tort Claims Act, or (C) bringing civil action under other law. Election is final and conclusive upon acceptance of award. Requires the Administrator to establish arbitration procedures within 45 days of enactment; arbitration election is binding and precludes judicial review. Provides that compensation under this Act shall not be considered income or resources for any purpose under any federal, state, or local law, including taxation, welfare, and public assistance, and prohibits states from decreasing assistance due to receipt of benefits.

Also waives state, local, and individual matching requirements for agricultural programs responding to the fire, setting Federal share at 100 percent.
